parsed_topics.name: wiki_precompilation
parsed_topics.description: The process of pre-compiling raw sources into a structured, indexed wiki to enable efficient querying and retrieval.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: The use of a combination of BM25, vector embeddings, and graph-based indexing to enhance retrieval accuracy and relevance.

parsed_topics.name: graph_exploitation
parsed_topics.description: Leveraging existing graph structures (e.g., wikilinks, ref:KEY, co-change signals) to avoid redundant extraction and improve retrieval efficiency.

parsed_topics.name: hierarchical_scoping
parsed_topics.description: Implementing hierarchical memory and retrieval structures (e.g., rooms, folders, per-domain wikis) to improve search precision and reduce noise.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: Tracking supersession and contradiction relationships between content versions to address issues of stale or outdated information.

parsed_topics.name: behavioral_edges
parsed_topics.description: Incorporating behavioral signals (e.g., git co-change) as new edge types in the knowledge graph to capture relationships not represented by manual links.

parsed_topics.name: retrieval_architecture
parsed_topics.description: Designing a multi-layered retrieval system that includes raw sources, pre-compiled wikis, and federated retrieval services for different domains.

parsed_topics.name: existing_infrastructure
parsed_topics.description: Leveraging existing tools and conventions (e.g., ref:KEY, .memories/, ollama-bridge) to support the implementation of smart RAG without reinventing the wheel.
